questions.prompt: What does SEBoK identify as defining characteristics of a system of systems (SoS)?
must_include: emergence / emergent mission-level behaviour from constituent interactions
questions.provenance: SEBoK v2.13 ch25 â€” this pack frames SoS by operational independence + emergence (constituents retain autonomy / pre-existing objectives), NOT the Maier 'managerial independence' wording.
must_not_include: a system of systems has a single owning authority that controls all constituents

questions.prompt: Why does SEBoK say SoS engineering relies on 'influence and intervention' rather than 'control and design'? Tie your answer to a defining SoS characteristic.
must_include: constituent systems retain operational independence / autonomy
no central command authority over constituents
align constituent behaviour with SoS goals via incentives / architecture
questions.provenance: SEBoK v2.13 ch25 ('influence and intervention replaces control and design')
must_not_include: the SoS integrator directly controls each constituent's design

questions.prompt: What is MBSE according to SEBoK, and how does it differ from document-centric SE?
must_include: models are the primary artifacts rather than text-based documents
models as the central / authoritative technical baseline
questions.provenance: SEBoK v2.13 ch09 (Model-Based Systems Engineering; primary MBSE chapter) — ch06 (representing systems) also covers MBSE per the SKILL.md topic index
must_not_include: MBSE just means generating more documents automatically
